]

instance RobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment of the MABI MAX 100 robot system"
    base:contains MABIMAX100Robot, MotorComponent, GearboxComponent, ControllerComponent, EncoderComponent
]

instance MABIMAX100Encoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Encoder sensor for the MABI MAX 100 robot axes"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MQTTDataStream
]

instance MQTTDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MABIMAX100Encoder
    base:desc "Time-series sensor data streamed via MQTT protocol from physical robot components to digital shadow"
]

instance TimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from robot encoders"

    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to industry standards such as SysML [17] for model-based systems engineering, ACME [23], AADL [24], and MontiArc [25], [26] for architecture description languages (ADLs) to define component interfaces and interconnections. Methodologies including RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, and SMArDT are applied across abstraction levels (requirements, functional, logical, product/technical). Behavior modeling integrates Simulink [28], simulation tools [29], [30], and general-purpose languages (C++, Java), with interoperability ensured through heterogeneous stakeholder integration [31]."
]
